name: Accordion
description: Disclosure on native <details>/<summary> — keyboard, state and semantics come from the browser, zero JS shipped. Plus/minus text marker, hairline separators.

useCases: Long reference sections that would otherwise dominate the page (token tables on /system)
Progressive disclosure of secondary detail in authored pages

antiPatterns.scenario: Hiding primary content behind an accordion
antiPatterns.reason: Core content must be visible without interaction; disclosure is for depth, not for the main thread.
antiPatterns.alternative: Keep primary content in the flow; collapse only reference material.

antiPatterns.scenario: Rebuilding the disclosure with divs, ARIA and JS
antiPatterns.reason: Reimplements what <details> provides natively and adds a JS dependency the page must not have.
antiPatterns.alternative: This component — or plain <details> where no tokens are needed.

a11y: Native <details>/<summary>: toggle is keyboard-operable (Enter/Space) and exposed as a button with expanded state
Optional heading prop keeps collapsed sections in the document outline
State marker is a CSS pseudo-element — decorative, never read as content
Expand/collapse animates via motion.expand tokens (::details-content); unsupporting browsers toggle instantly, prefers-reduced-motion disables it
